hero.title: Understand different brain tumors made simple.
hero.description: This starter app helps patients and families explore common brain tumor diagnoses, symptoms, diagnosis, and treatment pathways. It is educational only and should be used alongside advice from your care team.

focusCard.label: What this app focuses on
focusCard.title: Plain-language summaries
focusCard.description: Fast explanations of what a tumor is, common symptoms, and how treatment decisions are usually made.

urgentCard.label: Get urgent help now if someone has
urgentCard.title: new seizures, severe confusion, fainting, or rapid weakness
urgentCard.description: These symptoms can be an emergency. Call emergency services or seek immediate care.

infoStrip.title: What is a brain tumor?
infoStrip.body: A brain tumor is an abnormal growth of cells in the brain or nearby structures. Some tumors are slow-growing and noncancerous; others are cancerous and grow more quickly.

infoStrip.title: Why types matter
infoStrip.body: Tumor type, grade, and location can all change symptoms, treatment, and follow-up needs. Two people with "brain tumors" may have very different care plans.

infoStrip.title: What doctors use to decide
infoStrip.body: MRI findings, surgery or biopsy results, tumor genetics, symptoms, and a patient's overall health all help guide treatment choices.

directory.title: Browse different brain tumor diagnoses
directory.description: Start with the search box or use the filter buttons to narrow the list.

careGuide.title: Helpful questions for a clinic visit

groups.title: About the diagnosis
items: What is the exact tumor type and grade?
Was the diagnosis confirmed with pathology?
Do molecular markers or genetic findings matter here?

groups.title: About treatment
items: Is surgery, radiation, medication, or monitoring recommended?
What is the goal of treatment right now?
What side effects should we prepare for?

groups.title: About daily life
items: What symptoms should make us call right away?
Are driving, work, school, or exercise restrictions needed?
Should we meet with neuro-oncology, rehab, or supportive care?

sources.title: Medical references used in this starter app
sources.description: Content was summarized from patient-facing or government medical sources and rewritten for readability.
